mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is positive when policy rates are falling from already-low levels and fails if cuts occur during acute deflationary stress that coincides with falling earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor income and household demand, which depresses equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest after a 0.3 percentage point or larger increase in unemployment within three months.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A combined decline in RSAFS and HOUST identifies weakening consumer demand and residential activity simultaneously, which is more bearish for equities than either series alone. When both measures deteriorate together, S&P 500 returns over the next quarter should be negative because revenue growth and cyclical risk appetite both weaken.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST weakening state does not outperform each single-series effect in sign accuracy, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of joint-weakening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates stronger real output without inflation pressure, supporting earnings margins and equity multiples. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when industrial production accelerates and price growth does not.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising INDPRO and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates stay suppressed and investors price continued monetary accommodation, which supports risk assets over the next 20 trading days. The signal is positive only in low-rate inflation scares that do not force immediate tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate inflation episodes are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if returns turn negative once FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent job losses in PAYEMS signal declining payroll income and weaker consumption, which should depress equities over the following quarter. The effect is strongest when PAYEMS growth is negative for at least two consecutive months, because the labor market deterioration becomes broad rather than noisy.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if two-month negative PAYEMS streaks are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A rising GDPC1 print after recessionary weakness marks a durable demand rebound, which should lift corporate revenue expectations and support S&P 500 gains over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP growth is revised away or when the rebound is offset by simultaneous labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in GDPC1 are not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of quarters, or if the rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized drop in RSAFS and HOUST after a strong prior expansion indicates demand exhaustion in consumption and housing, which should turn S&P 500 returns negative over the next week as cyclical sectors lead the market lower. The signal is weaker when only one series falls because the joint pattern better captures broad demand rollover.
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears outside late-cycle periods.
